catalog.Title: The Hobbit
catalog.Author: J.R.R. Tolkien
catalog.Description: Bilbo Baggins embarks on an unexpected journey with dwarves to reclaim treasure from a dragon.

catalog.Author: George Orwell
catalog.Description: Winston Smith lives in a dystopian world controlled by Big Brother where thought is policed.

catalog.Title: To Kill a Mockingbird
catalog.Author: Harper Lee
catalog.Description: Scout Finch learns about prejudice and justice in the Deep South during the 1930s.

catalog.Title: Pride and Prejudice
catalog.Author: Jane Austen
catalog.Description: Elizabeth Bennet navigates love, marriage, and social class in Regency England.

catalog.Title: The Great Gatsby
catalog.Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
catalog.Description: Jay Gatsby's obsessive pursuit of lost love amid 1920s excess and corruption.

catalog.Title: Sapiens
catalog.Author: Yuval Noah Harari
catalog.Description: History of humankind from the Stone Age to the 21st century and our cognitive revolution.

catalog.Title: Dune
catalog.Author: Frank Herbert
catalog.Description: Paul Atreides battles for control of the desert planet Arrakis and its valuable spice.

catalog.Title: Clean Code
catalog.Author: Robert C. Martin
catalog.Description: Practical guide to writing maintainable, readable code with real-world examples for developers.

catalog.Title: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
catalog.Author: J.K. Rowling
catalog.Description: Young wizard Harry discovers his magical heritage and battles dark forces at Hogwarts.

catalog.Title: The Da Vinci Code
catalog.Author: Dan Brown
catalog.Description: Robert Langdon uncovers religious conspiracies while solving cryptic puzzles in Paris.

catalog.Title: Atomic Habits
catalog.Author: James Clear
catalog.Description: Small changes for remarkable results through better habit formation and breaking bad ones.

catalog.Title: The Alchemist
catalog.Author: Paulo Coelho
catalog.Description: Santiago the shepherd follows his Personal Legend across deserts to find treasure.

catalog.Title: Brave New World
catalog.Author: Aldous Huxley
catalog.Description: In a technologically advanced future, society is engineered for stability at the cost of individuality.

catalog.Title: The Catcher in the Rye
catalog.Author: J.D. Salinger
catalog.Description: Holden Caulfield recounts his experiences in New York after being expelled from prep school.

catalog.Title: The Road
catalog.Author: Cormac McCarthy
catalog.Description: A father and son journey through a post-apocalyptic landscape seeking safety and hope.

catalog.Title: Moby-Dick
catalog.Author: Herman Melville
catalog.Description: Captain Ahab obsessively hunts the white whale Moby Dick, symbol of fate and vengeance.

catalog.Title: The Martian
catalog.Author: Andy Weir
catalog.Description: Stranded on Mars, astronaut Mark Watney fights to survive using ingenuity and engineering.

catalog.Title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
catalog.Author: Oscar Wilde
catalog.Description: A man remains youthful while his hidden portrait ages and reflects his moral decay.

catalog.Title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
catalog.Author: Stieg Larsson
catalog.Description: A journalist and hacker team up to uncover dark family secrets in Sweden.

catalog.Title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
catalog.Author: Daniel Kahneman
catalog.Description: Explores the two systems that drive human thought: fast intuition and slow reasoning.

catalog.Title: The Name of the Wind
catalog.Author: Patrick Rothfuss
catalog.Description: A gifted young man grows into the world’s most legendary wizard, telling his own story.

catalog.Title: The Fellowship of the Ring
catalog.Author: J.R.R. Tolkien
catalog.Description: Frodo Baggins begins his quest to destroy the One Ring in Middle-earth’s epic struggle.

catalog.Title: Fahrenheit 451
catalog.Author: Ray Bradbury
catalog.Description: In a future where books are banned, a fireman begins to question his role in suppressing knowledge.

catalog.Title: The Silence of the Lambs
catalog.Author: Thomas Harris
catalog.Description: An FBI trainee seeks help from imprisoned killer Hannibal Lecter to catch another murderer.

catalog.Title: The Kite Runner
catalog.Author: Khaled Hosseini
catalog.Description: A touching story of friendship, betrayal, and redemption set in Afghanistan’s turbulent history.

catalog.Title: Life of Pi
catalog.Author: Yann Martel
catalog.Description: A boy is stranded on a lifeboat with a Bengal tiger after a shipwreck, discovering inner strength.

catalog.Title: The Shining
catalog.Author: Stephen King
catalog.Description: A struggling writer becomes the winter caretaker of a haunted hotel as madness consumes him.

catalog.Title: A Game of Thrones
catalog.Author: George R.R. Martin
catalog.Description: Noble families vie for power in a harsh medieval world where winters can last decades.

catalog.Title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
catalog.Author: Mark Manson
catalog.Description: A candid self-help book about focusing on what truly matters and embracing life's struggles.

catalog.Title: Educated
catalog.Author: Tara Westover
catalog.Description: A memoir of a woman who escapes her strict survivalist upbringing to pursue higher education.

catalog.Title: The Outsiders
catalog.Author: S.E. Hinton
catalog.Description: Teenage outsiders navigate class conflict and loyalty in a 1960s coming-of-age drama.

catalog.Title: The Old Man and the Sea
catalog.Author: Ernest Hemingway
catalog.Description: An aging fisherman battles a giant marlin in a timeless tale of endurance and dignity.

catalog.Title: Crime and Punishment
catalog.Author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
catalog.Description: A destitute student wrestles with guilt and morality after committing a murder in St. Petersburg.

catalog.Title: The Handmaid's Tale
catalog.Author: Margaret Atwood
catalog.Description: In a totalitarian future, women are stripped of rights, and one struggles to survive in servitude.

catalog.Title: Neuromancer
catalog.Author: William Gibson
catalog.Description: A washed-up hacker is hired for one last job in a fast-paced world of cyberpunk and AIs.

catalog.Title: Slaughterhouse-Five
catalog.Author: Kurt Vonnegut
catalog.Description: Billy Pilgrim becomes 'unstuck in time,' experiencing war, aliens, and existential absurdity.

catalog.Title: The Midnight Library
catalog.Author: Matt Haig
catalog.Description: A woman explores infinite lives in a mysterious library where every book is a different version of her story.

catalog.Title: The Wind-Up Bird Chronicle
catalog.Author: Haruki Murakami
catalog.Description: A Tokyo man’s search for his missing wife leads him into surreal worlds of memory and fate.

catalog.Title: The Stranger
catalog.Author: Albert Camus
catalog.Description: An indifferent man faces the absurdity of existence and consequence after a senseless crime.

catalog.Title: The Shadow of the Wind
catalog.Author: Carlos Ruiz Zafón
catalog.Description: A boy in post-war Barcelona discovers a mysterious author and a hidden world of forgotten books.

catalog.Title: The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo
catalog.Author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
catalog.Description: An aging film icon recounts her glamorous and scandalous life to a young journalist.

catalog.Title: Project Hail Mary
catalog.Author: Andy Weir
catalog.Description: A man wakes up alone in space with no memory, tasked with saving humanity from extinction.

catalog.Title: Ready Player One
catalog.Author: Ernest Cline
catalog.Description: A teenager enters a virtual reality contest that holds the key to immense fortune and control of the digital world.

catalog.Title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
catalog.Author: Rebecca Skloot
catalog.Description: The true story of the woman whose cells revolutionized medical research without her knowledge.

catalog.Title: The Power of Habit
catalog.Author: Charles Duhigg
catalog.Description: Explores how habits form, how they can be changed, and their influence on our behavior and success.

catalog.Title: Born a Crime
catalog.Author: Trevor Noah
catalog.Description: A comedian’s memoir of growing up in apartheid South Africa as the child of a black mother and white father.

catalog.Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
catalog.Author: Douglas Adams
catalog.Description: Earth is demolished to make way for a hyperspace bypass, sending one man on a cosmic adventure of absurdity.

catalog.Title: Meditations
catalog.Author: Marcus Aurelius
catalog.Description: A collection of philosophical writings by a Roman emperor on virtue, acceptance, and the art of living well.

catalog.Title: The Invisible Man
catalog.Author: H.G. Wells
catalog.Description: A scientist becomes invisible and slowly descends into madness and violence.

catalog.Title: Dune Messiah
catalog.Author: Frank Herbert
catalog.Description: The continuation of Paul Atreides’s rule as emperor grappling with destiny and sacrifice on Arrakis.

catalog.Title: Thinking in Systems
catalog.Author: Donella H. Meadows
catalog.Description: An accessible guide to understanding complex systems and how feedback loops shape real-world outcomes.

catalog.Title: Man’s Search for Meaning
catalog.Author: Viktor E. Frankl
catalog.Description: A Holocaust survivor’s profound reflection on finding purpose through suffering and resilience.
